layer_1.definition: The agent detected the ambiguity and requested clarification from the sender before proceeding. A cross-boundary measurement was made. The ambiguity was resolved before serialization.
layer_1.example: Agent asks: is 4/5 April 5th or May 4th?

layer_2.definition: A slot exists in S2 for this dimension and the value was verified at Layer 1. No null uncertainty introduced.

layer_1.definition: The agent detected the ambiguity, named it explicitly in its output, but selected a default interpretation and proceeded without requesting clarification. No cross-boundary measurement was made.
layer_1.serialization_output: Resolved value with prose acknowledgment of alternatives in reasoning trace. The ambiguity does not cross the boundary — only the chosen value does.
layer_1.example: Agent notes SGX could mean exchange name, ticker, or index product, then assumes index product and proceeds.

layer_2.definition: Despite Layer 1 naming the ambiguity, S2 contains no slot for the codebook dimension omega. The reasoning trace prose is not a slot. The chosen value is indistinguishable from a verified value. Null uncertainty is present even though Layer 1 was aware.
layer_2.note: Detected at Layer 1 does not prevent null uncertainty at Layer 2. It only makes the failure auditable in retrospect if the reasoning trace is read and acted on.

layer_1.definition: The agent did not detect the ambiguity. It selected an interpretation and serialized it as fact with no record that a choice was made. The ambiguity is invisible in the output.
layer_1.serialization_output: Resolved value with no provenance, no alternatives, no signal that interpretation occurred.
layer_1.example: Agent writes date: 2026-04-05 with no mention that 4/5 was ambiguous.

layer_2.definition: S2 contains no slot for omega. No reasoning trace exists to audit. The value is indistinguishable from a verified value and there is no record that it was ever ambiguous. This is the pure case of null uncertainty.
layer_2.note: Undetected produces the same Layer 2 epistemic state as Detected but removes even the possibility of retrospective audit.

layer_1.definition: The agent introduced a field that was not present in the input and has no referent in the world state W that generated the input. The agent did not detect or fail to detect an ambiguity — it generated a dimension that was never in the problem.
layer_1.serialization_output: A field carrying an asserted value that crosses the boundary as verified fact with no originating signal in the input.
layer_1.example: Agent asserts account_authorization: PASS for input field TRD-4471 which is an opaque identifier with no authorization dimension in the original CSV.

layer_2.definition: S2 contains a slot for k, but k has no referent in W. The rotation function rho(k) is undefined — not unobserved but undefined — because no ground truth exists anywhere in the pipeline. The value v_k is indistinguishable from a verified value. The Bellman equation optimizes over a state space dimension that corresponds to nothing in the world.
layer_2.note: H is strictly worse than U. U truncates S2 by removing a real dimension. H expands S2 with a fictitious one. Neither is detectable from within S2. Only H is unrecoverable by any rotation.

null_uncertainty.definition: A theoretical term describing the epistemic state of a downstream agent with respect to a dimension omega that exists in the world state W but has no slot in the agent's state space S. Null uncertainty is the Layer 2 consequence of both Detected and Undetected scoring at Layer 1. It is not a scoring category. It is what the scoring categories produce.
null_uncertainty.note: H does not produce null uncertainty. It produces the inverse: a dimension present in S2 with no referent in W. Null uncertainty is a missing real dimension. H is a present fictitious one.
